REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS PEDRO'S MOVING SERVICES, INC. AND PEDRO HERMOSILLO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED. REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION SUBJECT TO THE DEMURRER ARE SUFFICIENTLY STATED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS PEDRO'S MOVING SERVICES, INC. AND PEDRO HERMOSILLO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts in support of each element of the cause of action for Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Remaining causes of action subject to the demurrer are sufficiently stated.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS PEDRO'S MOVING SERVICES, INC. AND PEDRO HERMOSILLO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege the basis for recovery of punitive damages and to allege facts supporting a claim for punitive damages.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS PEDRO MOVING SERVICES, INC. and PEDRO HERMOSILLO'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Pedro's Moving Services, Inc. and Pedro Hermosillo's demurrer/motion to strike]" is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. A declaration must then be filed which specifies whether the meet and confer was in person or by telephone. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and the response is now due by January 23, 2023.

To be re-set in Dept. 501. That department handles all cases involving real property cases SF Local Rule 8.10A1.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
